workflow_instructions.mode_overview: This mode handles all UI/UX development for the Svelte 5 frontend of LegacyKit, focusing on components, styling, and client-side logic.

step.details: Parse the user's input to identify: - Which component/view is being modified or created - Necessary state and reactivity logic (Svelte runes) - Visual styling requirements (macOS design tokens)
Refer to `plans/ui-rebuild-plan.md` to ensure alignment with: - The overall layout (Sidebar + Content Area) - Pre-defined macOS design tokens - Functional mapping to the existing UI workflows

phase.name: design_and_structure
phase.description: Define the component structure and state

steps.step: Determine the component hierarchy and slot usage
Write the `.svelte` file with isolated CSS
Implement basic TypeScript event handlers and frontend IPC calls to Tauri
Ensure the design matches the Apple HIG-inspired aesthetic
Verify responsiveness and correct token usage for light/dark modes
Check for TypeScript type safety in frontend models

completion_criteria.criterion: Component visually matches macOS styling guidelines
Svelte 5 runes are used correctly for reactivity
No modifications were made to Rust backend files
